title: Are you local?
desc: Someone mistakes you for a native
setting: At a bus stop, a tourist asks you for directions in halting German, assuming you're a local.

steps.speaker: Tourist
steps.es: Entschuldigung, wüssed Sie, wo de Löwedänkmal isch?
steps.en: Excuse me, do you know where the Lion Monument is?

options.es: Ja klar! Da dure und denn linggs, öppe fümf Minute.
options.en: Yes of course! Through there and then left, about five minutes.
options.feedback: Confident, local, and helpful — they'd never guess you're not from here.

options.es: Ähm, ich bi au nöd sicher.
options.feedback: If you do know it, own it — you've earned the local's confidence.
Ironic, since they took you for a native! If you know the way, just help.

steps.speaker: Tourist
steps.es: Oh super, merci! Sie kenned sich ja guet uus.
steps.en: Oh great, thanks! You know your way around well.

options.es: Ja, ich wohne da. S'isch e schöni Stadt, gäll.
options.en: Yes, I live here. It's a beautiful city, isn't it.
options.feedback: Easy local pride — and a quiet thrill at being seen as one of them.
Modest and true — a nice, grounded reply.

options.es: Eigentli bin ich au nöd lang da.
options.en: Actually I haven't been here long either.
options.feedback: No need to downplay it — to them, you're as local as they come.

steps.speaker: Tourist
steps.es: Chönd Sie mer no es guets Restaurant empfehle?
steps.en: Could you recommend a good restaurant too?

options.es: Gärn! Probiered s'Wirtshuus a de Reuss, das isch fein.
options.en: Gladly! Try the inn by the Reuss, it's lovely.
options.feedback: A real local tip delivered with ease. You're the expert now.

options.es: Es git viili, chunnt druf aa, was Sie möged.
options.en: There are many, depends on what you like.
options.feedback: Thoughtful and helpful — a natural follow-up question would seal it.

options.es: Kei Ahnig, suechid Sie im Internet.
options.en: No idea, look it up online.
options.feedback: Dismissive when you could shine — offer even one place you like.

steps.speaker: Tourist
steps.es: Sie sind sehr fründlich. Vielä Dank und schöne Tag!
steps.en: You're very friendly. Many thanks and have a nice day!

options.es: Bitte gärn, viel Spass z'Luzärn! Tschüss.
options.en: You're very welcome, enjoy Lucerne! Bye.
options.feedback: Warm, local, complete. A lovely full-circle moment for you.
Gracious and natural — exactly what a local would say.
A bit curt after such thanks — a warmer send-off costs nothing.
